name: Mining (Zimbabwe)
definition: Businesses extracting and beneficiating minerals, from small-scale and artisanal operations to large mechanised mines, together with directly related contract mining services.

subSectors.name: Gold mining
subSectors.description: Primary and small-scale gold producers and custom millers.

subSectors.name: Base metals and ferroalloys
subSectors.description: Nickel, chrome, and related smelting feed operations.

subSectors.name: Coal and energy minerals
subSectors.description: Coal producers supplying power and industry.

subSectors.name: Small-scale and artisanal mining
subSectors.description: Registered small-scale miners and custom milling.

subSectors.name: Contract mining and drilling services
subSectors.description: Drilling, blasting, load-and-haul and exploration services.

typicalBusinessSizes.description: Artisanal and small-scale workings, often seasonal.
Registered small-scale mines with basic plant.
Established mines with formal payroll and processing plant.
Mechanised corporate mines and multi-site producers.

officialSizeThresholds.note: Official small-scale/large-scale mining classifications were not verified from an official source in this revision; source the current Mines and Minerals framework definitions before product use.

notes: Descriptive segmentation for product design; not an official classification. Mineral marketing and royalty regimes vary by mineral and are declared as research gaps rather than described.
